message:
feat: add Cline and Amp providers; expand test matrix and fix CLI list semantics

New providers:

- Cline (src/providers/cline.rs, 719 lines): Full Provider trait
  implementation for the VS Code extension (saoudrizwan.claude-dev).
  Reads api_conversation_history.json and falls back to ui_messages.json.
  Writer produces task directories with api_conversation_history.json,
  ui_messages.json, task_metadata.json, and updates state/taskHistory.json.
  Detection probes CLINE_HOME env override and VS Code/Cursor/VSCodium
  globalStorage paths. CLI alias: cln.

- Amp (src/providers/amp.rs, 656 lines): Full Provider trait
  implementation for Amp's centralized thread JSON store. Reads thread
  files with T-<uuid> naming convention, extracts workspace from
  env.initial.cwd or workspace tree URIs (including file:// decoding).
  Parses tool_use/tool_result blocks and info-role summary messages.
  Writer produces Amp-native thread JSON with env/workspace wiring.
  Detection probes AMP_HOME, AMP_DATA_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME, and legacy
  VS Code globalStorage paths. CLI alias: amp.

Existing provider changes:

- Aider (src/providers/aider.rs): Filter metadata-only tool output lines
  (model and workspace lines) from being pushed into tool_lines, so they
  only appear as extracted metadata rather than duplicated in message
  content. Updated atomic_write call to pass provider slug.

- Claude Code, Codex, Gemini (claude_code.rs, codex.rs, gemini.rs):
  Updated atomic_write/restore_backup calls to thread provider slug for
  accurate error reporting (fixes bd-1fb).

Pipeline and discovery improvements:

- pipeline.rs: atomic_write() and restore_backup() now accept a
  provider_slug parameter, propagated into all SessionWriteError
  instances. All test call sites updated. This eliminates empty provider
  strings in error messages.

- discovery.rs: Improved --source <path> resolution when the path is
  outside any provider's session roots. Instead of blindly using the
  first installed provider, the registry now: (1) tries lightweight file
  signature inference (vscdb -> cursor, JSONL first-line heuristics for
  codex/claude-code, JSON shape for gemini/codex), (2) probes all
  providers by attempting read_session and selects the most plausible
  parser based on message count and role diversity. Adds
  is_plausible_session() helper and infer_provider_for_path() method.

- Registered Cline and Amp in ProviderRegistry::default_registry().

CLI improvements (src/main.rs):

- Implemented --workspace filtering for `casr list`: sessions are now
  filtered by workspace path prefix match, with tilde expansion support.

- Implemented --sort semantics for `casr list`: supports "date"
  (default, descending), "messages" (descending), and "provider"
  (alphabetical) with secondary sort tiebreakers. Unknown sort values
  produce actionable error messages.

- Refactored list internals from serde_json::Value to typed
  SessionSummary struct for cleaner sorting/filtering logic.

- Updated CLI description and help text to list all 8 providers.

Test additions:

- Test fixtures: New synthetic fixtures for Cline
  (tasks/1700001234567/api_conversation_history.json + taskHistory.json),
  Amp (T-aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa.json), and Aider
  (aider_simple.md). Expected canonical summaries for each
  (expected/cline_simple.json, amp_simple.json, aider_simple.json).
  fixtures_manifest.json updated with all three entries.

- fixtures_test.rs: Added fixture_cline_simple, fixture_amp_simple,
  fixture_aider_simple reader validation tests.

- cli_e2e_test.rs: Added CC->Cline, Cline->CC, CC->Amp, Amp->CC,
  CC->Aider, Aider->CC conversion tests. Added --workspace filter and
  --sort messages CLI integration tests. Updated env isolation to include
  CLINE_HOME, AIDER_HOME, AMP_HOME, XDG_CONFIG_HOME, XDG_DATA_HOME.

- json_contract_test.rs: Updated provider count assertions from 6 to 8.
  Added cline/amp slug and alias assertions. Updated env isolation.

- roundtrip_test.rs: Added CC->Cline, Cline->CC, CC->Amp, Amp->CC
  round-trip fidelity tests with env mutex guards.

- writer_test.rs: Added Cline and Amp writer tests verifying written
  artifact structure, read-back fidelity, and file layout.

- discovery_test.rs: Updated MockProvider with orphan path read support.
  Renamed test to reflect new best-effort provider selection behavior.

- scripts/e2e_test.sh: Added CLINE_HOME, AIDER_HOME, AMP_HOME,
  OPENCODE_HOME env setup. Added CC->Cline, Cline->CC, CC->Amp,
  Amp->CC, CC->Aider, Aider->CC, CC->OpenCode, OpenCode->CC e2e paths.
  Updated provider listing assertions and reset_env cleanup.

Other:

- .ubsignore: New file for UBS ignore patterns.
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Closed bd-20x.4 epic (all additional providers
  complete), bd-20x.4.2 (Cline), bd-20x.4.4 (Amp), bd-20x.4.6
  (shared fixture matrix). Added and closed bd-1fb (provider slug in
  write errors), bd-1od (source path resolution), bd-qlg (list
  workspace/sort). README.md updated with CLINE_HOME, AIDER_HOME,
  AMP_HOME env var documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1kg","title":"EPIC: CLI Interface & UX","description":"Implement the casr CLI using clap.

Primary usage pattern: `casr <target-agent> resume <session-id>`.

Target agents:
- `cc` (Claude Code)
- `cod` (Codex)
- `gmi` (Gemini CLI)

Additional subcommands:
- `casr list` (list sessions across providers)
- `casr info <session-id>` (inspect a session without converting)
- `casr providers` (detection status + evidence)
- `casr completions <shell>`

The CLI must:
- auto-detect the source provider from the session ID (with explicit ambiguity errors)
- show clear progress + actionable failures
- report success with the exact resume command for the target provider
- support `--json` output for automation

Uses color with TTY detection; colors must be disabled automatically for `--json` and non-TTY output.

## Success Criteria

- Primary `resume` flow is fast, safe (atomic writes + backup), and self-verifying.
- `list/info/providers` are useful for discovery and debugging without conversion.
- All commands support `--json` with stable schemas and contract tests.
- Error messages include remediation hints (install, `--source`, `--force`, etc.).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:09.242303221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.594813342Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.594733863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.135990126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.056337066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.1","title":"Implement CLI argument parsing with clap","description":"Implement the clap CLI in `src/main.rs`. Use clap derive macros.

## Commands

Usage patterns:

- `casr <target> resume <session-id>` (primary)
- `casr list [--provider <slug>] [--workspace <path>] [--limit <n>] [--sort <field>]`
- `casr info <session-id>`
- `casr providers`
- `casr completions <bash|zsh|fish>`
- `casr --version`
- `casr --help`

Where `<target>` is one of: `cc` (Claude Code), `cod` (Codex), `gmi` (Gemini CLI).

## Global Flags

- `--dry-run` : show what would happen without writing.
- `--force` : overwrite existing target session if it exists (creates backups).
- `--json` : machine-readable JSON output (no ANSI color, stable field names).
- `--verbose` : enable debug-level logs.
- `--trace` : enable trace-level logs.
- `--source <alias_or_path>` : pin source provider alias (`cc|cod|gmi`) OR a direct native session file path.
- `--enrich` : opt-in enrichment messages (bd-ikb.4).

Logging note:
- `--verbose`/`--trace` must configure the tracing subscriber filter directly (do not claim to "set RUST_LOG" at runtime).
- If `RUST_LOG` is already set, `--verbose`/`--trace` should override only for the `casr` target (documented precedence).

## Acceptance Criteria

- clap surface matches the README command reference.
- `--source` accepts either a known alias or a path; invalid values produce actionable usage errors.
- `--json` mode is supported across commands and disables color automatically.
- `--verbose` and `--trace` are wired to tracing subscriber filtering (bd-2zw.6).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:48.436160082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619805733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619702089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.421300610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.554982969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.2","title":"Implement resume command output and UX","description":"Implement the user-facing output for `casr <target> resume <session-id>`. This is what the user sees when they run the tool.

Success output example:

  ✓ Found session 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
    Source: Codex (codex)
    Workspace: /data/projects/myapp
    Messages: 47 (23 user, 24 assistant)
    Duration: 2h 15m

  ✓ Converted to Claude Code format
    Written to: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/new-session-id.jsonl
    Verified: read-back check passed ✓

  Resume with:
    claude --resume new-session-id

Backup notification (when `--force` was used and an existing session was backed up):

  ⚠ Existing session backed up to ~/.claude/projects/abc123/old-session-id.jsonl.bak

Error output examples:

  ✗ Session ID 'bad-id' not found
    Searched: Claude Code (roots: 2, files: 23), Codex (roots: 1, files: 156), Gemini (roots: 1, files: 12)
    Tip: Run `casr list` to see available sessions
    Tip: Use `--source <provider>` if you know the origin

  ✗ Target provider not writable
    Provider: Claude Code
    Evidence: ~/.claude/projects is read-only
    Tip: Fix permissions or set `CLAUDE_HOME` to a writable location

  ✗ Session already exists in target
    Existing: ~/.claude/projects/abc123/existing-session-id.jsonl (47 messages, 2h ago)
    Use `--force` to overwrite (creates .bak), or resume existing:
      claude --resume existing-session-id

  ✗ Write verification failed (this is a bug in casr)
    The written file could not be read back. Please report this with `--trace --json` output.

UX requirements:
- Use `colored` crate for colors (TTY-aware).
- Show progress for large sessions.
- In `--json` mode, output structured JSON instead of human text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Success output includes: source provider, workspace, message counts, written paths, and resume command.
- Warnings (validation warnings, binary-missing warnings) are visible in human output and present in `--json` result.
- Errors are actionable and include remediation hints.
- Output formatting is deterministic enough for e2e tests (bd-24z.5/bd-24z.6).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:00.316169403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.902957521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:43.902863214Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.674318857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.623081923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:04:16.265126132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1kg.4","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"Use `clap_complete` to generate shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish. Add a `casr completions <shell>` subcommand that writes completion scripts to stdout.

Instead, infer the owning provider via lightweight file signature heuristics and/or probing installed providers to select the best match deterministically.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:43:09.312330539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:48:32.850343566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:48:32.850323148Z","close_reason":"Improved SourceHint::Path resolution: if path not under any provider root, infer provider via lightweight file signature (json/jsonl/vscdb) or probe providers and pick most plausible parser; updated discovery unit test; cargo fmt/clippy/test pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","discovery"]}
 {"id":"bd-20x","title":"EPIC: Provider Readers (Import from Native Format)","description":"Implement readers that parse each provider's native session format into the canonical IR. Each reader takes a session file path and produces a `CanonicalSession`.

Scope:
- Claude Code (`~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl/.json/.claude`)
- Codex (`~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` + legacy JSON)
- Gemini CLI (`~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/session-*.json`)

Port and adapt connector parsing logic from CASS (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/`) for behavior parity.

Critical architecture constraint:
- casr MUST vendor/copy/adapt relevant CASS logic directly into casr source.
- casr MUST NOT depend on CASS as an external runtime/library dependency.

Reader acceptance requirements:
1. Handle all known format variants (JSONL vs JSON, string vs block-array content, mixed timestamp formats).
2. Preserve provider-specific non-canonical fields in `session.metadata` and `message.extra`.
3. Be permissive on malformed lines/events (skip with diagnostics where safe) and fail only on unrecoverable session corruption.
4. Produce deterministic output ordering and stable parsing behavior for tests.
5. Emit trace-level diagnostics at key decisions (role mapping, timestamp fallback path, workspace inference path).

This epic is the import half of the conversion pipeline.

## Success Criteria

-{"id":"bd-20x.4","title":"Add support for additional providers (Cline, Amp, Aider, Cursor)","description":"After the initial three providers (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) are complete and stable, extend casr to additional providers where formats are known or can be reverse-engineered.

 {"id":"bd-24z","title":"EPIC: Testing & Validation","description":"Comprehensive testing and validation for casr.

Required coverage dimensions:
1. Unit tests for each reader and writer.
2. Round-trip invariants (`read->canonical->write->read`) including per-provider and cross-provider paths.
3. Integration tests using realistic fixture corpora for all supported providers.
4. Property-based tests for normalization and parse/serialize resilience.
5. End-to-end CLI tests and shell e2e scripts with detailed structured logging.
6. Real-provider smoke checks (opt-in) that validate resume acceptance with installed provider CLIs.
7. JSON contract tests for `--json` output schemas and error envelopes.
8. Failure-injection tests for atomic-write + rollback semantics.
9. CASS parity regression tests for reader behavior on canonical fixture sets.

Non-negotiable logging/debuggability requirements:
- Every failing test must surface enough context to debug without reproducing manually.
- e2e scripts must output command, expected/actual, artifact paths, and pass/fail summary.
- `VERBOSE`/`TRACE` modes must be test-covered and produce inspectable diagnostics.

Key invariant:
For each provider `P`, `read_P(write_P(canonical))` should be equivalent to canonical (modulo documented provider-specific lossy fields).

## Success Criteria

- CI runs unit, round-trip, contracts, conformance, parity, e2e, and perf suites with actionable artifacts.
- A failing test provides enough logs/artifacts to root-cause without re-running locally.
- Writer correctness is validated by both reader round-trip and golden-output conformance (symmetric-bug protection).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:22.669687933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T16:31:45.703401668Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:31:45.703321047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.290967374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.213287848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-24z.1","title":"Create test fixtures for each provider format","description":"Create a high-fidelity fixture corpus under tests/fixtures/ for provider readers, writers, round-trip tests, and e2e harnesses.

Fixture strategy (MANDATORY):
1. Synthetic fixtures: minimal and edge-case sessions crafted by hand.
2. Redacted-real fixtures: sanitized snapshots derived from real provider session files so writer/reader behavior is anchored to real-world structures.

Provider-native fixture sets:
- Claude Code: simple, complex (content arrays/tool events), missing-workspace, unicode.
- Codex: modern JSONL, legacy JSON, token_count events, reasoning events.
- Gemini: simple, grounding/citations, missing-workspace inference.
- Cross-cutting edge fixtures: empty/whitespace content, null/missing timestamps, long message bodies, single-sided conversations.

Corpus rules:
- Keep provider fixture files strictly provider-native JSON/JSONL (no inline comment lines).
- Put fixture intent and caveats in sidecar notes files and a manifest (e.g., fixtures_manifest.json).
- For each fixture, include expected canonical summary output used by tests (message count/order/roles, workspace, metadata expectations, known lossy fields).
- Include at least one fixture per provider that exercises malformed-line tolerance without breaking parse of valid remaining messages.

Logging/debuggability requirements:
- Tests consuming fixtures must print fixture ID/path when failing.
- Mismatch reports must include expected vs actual canonical summary artifact paths.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fixture corpus includes both synthetic and redacted-real sets for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini.
- Every fixture has a sidecar expected canonical summary and documented intent.
- No provider-native fixture relies on non-native comment syntax.
- Fixture IDs are stable and referenced by reader/writer/round-trip/e2e tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:56.641976155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:41.512839113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:41.512621646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24z.1","depends_on_id":"bd-24z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:13.061662605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24z.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:07.867670879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ikb.4","title":"Implement session context enrichment for better resumption","description":"When converting a session from provider A to provider B, the target agent may benefit from additional context explaining that a conversion happened.  {"id":"bd-ikb.6","title":"Handle same-provider conversion gracefully","description":"Handle the edge case where the user runs `casr cc resume <claude-session-id>`: converting a session back to the same provider. This is not an error, but it is a special case.

Smart default behavior:
1. If source and target are the same provider and the session already exists at the expected native path, treat it as a no-op lookup: print resume command and exit.
2. If source and target are the same provider but the session is in a different location/shape (e.g., non-standard path via `--source`), treat it as a copy operation (read -> canonical -> write) under a new id.
3. Always print a notice: `Note: source and target are both <Provider>.`

Rationale:
- user doesn't remember which provider a session came from
- user wants the resume command without conversion
- user wants to duplicate/move a session inside a provider

Implementation sketch:
- In `ConversionPipeline::convert()`, detect `source.slug == target.slug` and choose between no-op vs copy depending on whether write is necessary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Same-provider no-op path produces correct resume command without writes.
- Same-provider copy path produces a new session artifact under the provider's native store.
- Behavior is covered by pipeline unit tests (bd-24z.9) and e2e script (bd-24z.6).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:10.140744557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:49.852692906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:49.852617615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.646929414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:14.399844997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
